title: isocoma menziesii var. sedoides
intro: Isocoma menziesii var. sedoides, also known as creeping isocoma or creeping yellow balsam root, is a perennial shrub in the Asteraceae family native to the western United States and Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is rich in isocombin, a compound with potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving immune function. The leaves and roots of Isocoma menziesii var. sedoides can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
